accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and the SBIC/SBA context is accurate per the Small Business Investment Act of 1958.

no_hallucinations.why: Added context (SBIC acronym, SBA administration) is correct and nothing is fabricated or contradicts the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill amends the SBI Act to exclude certain investments from SBIC maximum leverage limits.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Identifies the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 and describes amendments to private capital definition and leverage limits.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the government-funds exclusion with carve-outs for pension plans/university endowments and the 50%-or-$125M cap and prospective applicability.

effective_dates.why: Notes the prospective applicability (post-enactment investments) and the May 19, 2026 approval date.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the leverage exclusion categories, revised leverage limits, private capital redefinition, and the exclusion cap.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive without advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent across its three paragraphs.

concise.why: Free of padding and repetition while covering the operative provisions.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names SBICs, the SBA program context, and the Small Business Investment Act of 1958.

correct_figures.why: All figures ($300M→$200M, $250M, $175M, $475M, $350M, 50%/$125M) match the bill text exactly.
